review_level_reason: Auto-selected: 4 work packages plus multi-stage refinement loop crosses the full-review threshold.

summary.problem: The parallel-explore skill emits a synthesis only to chat scrollback. The matrix and per-branch findings have no persistent **structured artifact**, and there's no round-trip from synthesis to next action.
key_decisions: D1: Emit synthesis.json as the canonical structured output; derive synthesis.md and synthesis.html from it.
D2: HTML is a single inline file with system fonts; no external assets.
D3: Renderer is a separate sync CLI invoked by the skill after JSON is written.
summary.verification_approach: Three-layer JSON validation (structural → per-field → cross-field) with fail-loud stderr, plus integration tests on byte-identical determinism and attribute escaping.
constraint_highlights: Must not introduce npm dependencies — Node built-ins plus bounded markdown-mini only.
Must not break existing parallel-explore tests when the skill is reorganized.
Renderer output must be byte-identical for the same input + same --output-dir.

flagged_items.title: JSON is the renderer's source of truth; markdown is canonical-human; HTML is disposable
flagged_items.context:
Per the framing decision, the **structured JSON** is authoritative. The renderer derives both `synthesis.md` and `synthesis.html` from it.

- `synthesis.json` — emitted by the skill, validated by the renderer
- `synthesis.md` — git-trackable, ingestion-friendly
- `synthesis.html` — viewing surface, disposable

Confirm this layering before we commit to the renderer's contract.

flagged_items.title: Operators want a clipboard round-trip, not in-place editing
flagged_items.context:
I'm assuming the operator pattern is *open HTML → pick → click → paste*, not *open HTML → edit fields → save*. The 'pick this branch' button writes a follow-up prompt to clipboard. Local-edit-and-save is out of scope.

If the operator actually wants to *edit* the synthesis before sharing, this assumption changes the JS budget and the form model significantly.

flagged_items.title: Three-layer validation, fail-loud on stderr with grep-friendly tokens
flagged_items.context:
Renderer validation is **strict**:

1. Structural (top-level fields present, schema_version matches)
2. Per-field shape (types, ranges, enums)
3. Cross-field consistency (refs resolve, coverage complete)

All violations exit non-zero with stderr containing a unique token an operator can grep.

flagged_items.title: System fonts are good enough for visual fidelity
flagged_items.context:
No web font links. Relying on `system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, sans-serif` across the artifact.

If operators routinely view the artifact on devices where these fonts don't render acceptably (older Linux, specific browsers), we'd need to revisit. Decision driver: zero external assets.

flagged_items.title: Each renderer is per-skill, not a shared `/html` skill
flagged_items.context: Parallel-explore grid ≠ scorecard ≠ review gate. Targeted per-skill renderers produce high-fidelity HTML; a generic skill produces generic HTML. Restate: we will NOT build a shared `/html` skill in this experiment or the next.

flagged_items.title: Determinism is byte-identical, not whitespace-tolerant
flagged_items.context:
Two runs of the renderer on the same input + same `--output-dir` must produce **byte-identical** files (verified via SHA-256). This rules out timestamp comments, randomized DOM ids, and any clock reads.

If future template iterations make byte-identity hard to maintain, we'd weaken to canonical-form equality. Not in v1.

flagged_items.title: Synthesis HTML carries one round-trip action per branch
flagged_items.context:
Each branch tile has a 'Pick this branch' button. Click → copy a substituted prompt to clipboard. No multi-action menu, no per-section actions — one button per tile.

The prompt format is templated in the JSON (the skill's responsibility), not the renderer's. The renderer only substitutes & escapes.
